title: XoXaan
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}}
{{Character
|type=Sith
|image=[[File:XoXaan-headshot.jpg]]
|name=XoXaan
|homeworld=
|birth=
|death=Between [[6900 BBY]] and [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]]<ref name="Dates">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the [[Duel on Tatooine (Imperial Period)|duel on Tatooine]] depicted in ''[[The Life and Legend of Obi-Wan Kenobi]]'' to [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]]. Since the flashback sequences of [[Legacy (2006) 17|''Legacy'' (2006) 17]] take place soon after the aforementioned [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|duel]], the flashbacks—including the appearance of the [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]] of XoXaan, who lived in [[6900 BBY]] according to [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]—must take place around 17 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]].</ref>
|species=[[Humanoid/Legends|Humanoid]]<ref name="leg17">[[Legacy (2006) 17|''Legacy'' (2006) 17]]</ref>
|gender=
|pronouns=She/her<ref name="leg17" />
|height=
|mass=
|hair=[[Color/Legends|Black]]<ref name="leg17" />
|feathers=
|eyes=Gray<ref name="leg17" />
|skin=White<ref name="leg17" />
|cyber=
|families=
|parents=
|partners=
|siblings=
|children=
|affiliation=*[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]<ref name="leg30">[[Legacy (2006) 30|''Legacy'' (2006) 30]]</ref>
*[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]<ref name="leg30" />
*[[Black Legions]]<ref name="BOS">''[[Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side]]''</ref>
*[[Exiles]]<ref name="leg30" />
*[[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]]<ref name="leg30" />
**[[Sith Council]]<ref name="BOS" />
|masters=
|apprentices=[[Darth Krayt]]<ref name="leg17" />
}}
{{Quote|My brethren and I ''invented'' what it means to be a Sith. Are you willing to open yourself to that truth?|XoXaan|<ref name="leg17" />}}
'''XoXaan''' was a [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] who, along with eleven other [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Jedi defectors]], became the first [[Sith Lord/Legends|Lords of the Sith]]. As [[Marchioness]] of the Dark Jedi's [[Black Legions]], XoXaan fought in the cataclysmic [[Hundred-Year Darkness/Legends|Hundred-Year Darkness]] war but was defeated and [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exiled]] from the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] following a [[Battle of Corbos|rout]] on the [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Corbos]] in [[6900 BBY]]. Guilty of high [[Treason/Legends|treason]], XoXaan was one of twelve [[Exiles|Dark Jedi]] who were banished from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] only to [[Discovery of Korriban|discover]] the planet [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], where they found and [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] the [[Homeworld/Legends|aboriginal]] [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. Christened as the first of the Sith Lords, XoXaan and her contemporaries instituted a [[Sith/Legends|religion]] that centered around worship and practice of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]], and ruled their new [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]] as part of the [[Sith Council]] under the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], [[Ajunta Pall]].

Although other Sith Lords possessed similar talents, XoXaan was noted among her peers for her skills in [[Force healing/Legends|dark side healing]]. She recorded her knowledge in a [[XoXaan's holocron|holocron]], which was [[XoXaan's tomb|entombed]] with her in [[XoXaan's Temple|her temple]]. Her [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]] waited [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]] for an individual whom she felt was worthy of her Sith teachings, and it found one such [[Sith apprentice/Legends|student]] in the ex-Jedi [[Darth Krayt|A'Sharad Hett]] around [[17 BBY]]. From XoXaan's spirit and [[Sith holocron/Legends|holocron]], Hett learned the ways of the dark side and eventually assumed the mantle of Dark Lord under the moniker [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Krayt, creating a new [[One Sith|Sith Order]]. After his Sith [[Battle of Coruscant (Sith–Imperial War)|conquered]] the planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] in [[130 ABY]], Krayt erected a massive statue within the [[Temple of the Sith]] there in reverence of XoXaan—who was considered his first and greatest Sith [[Teacher/Legends|teacher]].

==Biography==
===Dissension and warfare===
{{Quote|The story begins not with Sith—but with ''fallen Jedi''. And a gathering of such warriors, nearly four thousand years ago—at the ''Battle of Corbos''. Many powerful figures fought there. ''Karness Muur. Remulus Dreypa. XoXaan.'' Rivals themselves—but allies against the light.|Kaliska, retelling tales of the Jedi|<ref name="Spiral 2">[[Lost Tribe of the Sith — Spiral 2|''Lost Tribe of the Sith — Spiral'' 2]]</ref>}}
[[File:Dark_Jedi_Corbos.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Marchioness XoXaan (left) alongside other Dark Jedi at the Battle of Corbos]]
A member of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] during the reign of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]], XoXaan was part of a [[Dark Jedi/Legends|group]] of beings within the Order who sought to further delve into [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] through exploration of its [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]. Those who espoused the new philosophy were opposed by proponents of the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side]], and their disagreement broke into a [[Hundred-Year Darkness/Legends|war]] that spanned one hundred [[Standard year/Legends|years]]. XoXaan [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanded]] the Dark Jedi's [[Black Legions]] as [[Marchioness]],<ref name="BOS" /> and in [[6900 BBY]],<ref name="JvS">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref> she and the other Dark Jedi mounted a climatic [[Battle of Corbos|battle]] against the Jedi Order on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Corbos]].<ref name="BOS" />

XoXaan fought on the surface of the world alongside fellow Dark Jedi [[Karness Muur]] and [[Baron/Legends|Baron]] [[Remulus Dreypa]],<ref name="Spiral 2" /> and her Black Legions mounted a charge at the battle. Despite their efforts,<ref name="BOS" /> with the Dark Jedi leadership's horrid [[Sithspawn/Legends|creatures]] requiring all the strength that the Jedi could muster to defeat,<ref name="Spiral 2" /> the Dark Jedi forces were ultimately routed, bringing the war to an end. She survived the conflict with eleven allies, including Dreypa, Muur, [[Sorzus Syn]], and leading [[High General]] [[Ajunta Pall]], all of whom were convicted of high [[Treason/Legends|treason]] by the Jedi Order. With [[Protosaber|lightsaber]] tips pointed at their backs, the Dark Jedi, rather than being [[Death/Legends|executed]], were marched aboard a [[Dark Jedi galleon|galleon]] headed beyond Republic territory; and so they were [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exiled]] into deep [[Realspace/Legends|space]],<ref name="BOS" /> left to contemplate the error of their ways.<ref name="GAOTS">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 3]]</ref>

===A newfound empire===
====Conquerors of darkness====
{{Quote|There were twelve of us, including several ranking commanders: High General Ajunta Pall; Marchioness XoXaan, commander of the Black Legions; ''[…]'' Other less distinguished Dark Jedi were prominent within our company only because they had not perished in the war.|Sorzus Syn, on the Exiles, ''Book of Sith''|<ref name="BOS" />}}
[[File:XoxaanMuur.jpg|thumb|right|250px|XoXaan and Karness Muur, worshipped by Korriban's native Sith species]]
Pursuing rumors of an ancient [[Sith Empire (Pre-Republic)|Kingdom of the Sith]], XoXaan and her fellow Dark Jedi,<ref name="BMF">{{BuildFalconCite|82|Guide to the Galaxy}}</ref> known as the [[Exiles]],<ref name="JvS" /> traveled into [[Sith Worlds/Legends|Sith Space]]. Along the [[Nache Bhelfia]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|route]], they discovered what would become the five sacred Sith worlds: [[Khar Delba]], [[Krayiss II|Krayiss]], [[Rhelg/Legends|Rhelg]], [[Ziost/Legends|Ziost]], and [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]].<ref name="BMF" /> In 6900 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]],<ref name="JvS" /> the Exiles [[Discovery of Korriban|arrived]] on the last and encountered the [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith people]].<ref name="BOS" />

After [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] spent deciphering the native culture, they installed themselves as [[Slavery/Legends|overlords]] of the Sith, with Ajunta Pall [[Sai cha/Legends|beheading]] the Sith ruler, [[Hakagram Graush]], in the presence of XoXaan, Syn, and Dreypa.<ref name="BOS" /> The Exiles were worshipped as [[Deity/Legends|gods]] known as the [[Jen'jidai]],<ref name="BMF" /> and Pall claimed dominion as [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]]; Xoxaan, along with other Exiles, became his [[Shadow Hand]]s<ref name="BOS" /> and took up the mantle of [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]], beginning the creation of the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]].<ref name="leg17" /> At one point on Korriban, Xoxaan appeared triumphant before the Sith people beside Muur.<ref name="leg30" />

At the [[Sith Citadel (Ziost)|Sith citadel]] on the empire's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] world of Ziost, XoXaan joined those with ambition or ranks to make up the new [[Sith Council]].<ref name="BOS" /> In her lifetime, XoXaan developed her [[Force healing/Legends|healing]] skills, for which she was renowned among the Sith.<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|III|343|XoXaan}}</ref> At one point, despite fellow Sith Lord Sorzus Syn's wish to study the [[Yoke of Seeming]]—a skin-[[Temperature/Legends|burning]] armature that allowed its wearer to take on whatever appearance they desired, and one of the most powerful [[Sith artifact/Legends|Sith artifacts]] Syn had encountered—XoXaan took the treasure for herself.<ref name="BOS" />

====Documentation and death====
{{Quote|My tomb was lain in the belly of this temple for long ages, my presence hidden from those who sought me—|XoXaan's spirit, regarding her burial|<ref name="leg17" />}}
[[File:ExiledDarkJedi-BoS.jpg|thumb|left|180px|XoXaan (leftmost) was among the Dark Jedi who became the first Sith Lords.]]
In her own [[Sorzus Syn's chronicle|chronicle]]—which was assembled into the ''[[Book of Sith]]'' by Sith Lord [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]] later, around [[19 BBY/Legends|19]] and [[18 BBY/Legends|18 BBY]]—Syn wrote of XoXaan's stubborness while recording information about the yoke. Syn also listed Marchioness XoXaan as a prominent co-founder of the Sith, and the chronicle included an illustration of XoXaan and her contemporaries triumphant over the Sith people, as well as an illustration of them at Graush's execution. However, those illustrations erroneously showed her with white hair.<ref name="BOS" />

Before XoXaan died, she recorded much of her knowledge within a [[XoXaan's holocron|Sith holocron]]. The Sith ruler was laid to rest along with her [[Sith holocron/Legends|holocron]] in [[XoXaan's tomb|her tomb]], which was in [[XoXaan's Temple|her temple]]. Although her flesh had died, the woman's [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]] resided within her holocron, its presence shielded from inquisitive outsiders. XoXaan was thus in waiting for an individual whom she felt had—like her—found the teachings of the light side to be lacking.<ref name="leg17" />

===Succeeding Sith===
{{Quote|I have waited for someone like you…someone who has served the light and found it empty, who has found how lacking the galaxy is. Someone who has known anger, hate…and despair.|XoXaan's spirit, to A'Sharad Hett|<ref name="leg17" />}}
Millennia after the Sith Empire's founding,<ref name="Spiral 2" /> between [[2975 BBY|2975]] and [[2974 BBY]],<ref name="MOREDATES">{{Faraway|url=comics/swlosttribeofthesithspiral/swlosttribeofthesithspiral1.html|text=''Star Wars: Lost Tribe of the Sith – Spiral'' #1}} — [[Lost Tribe of the Sith — Spiral 1|''Lost Tribe of the Sith—Spiral'' 1]] is set "one [[Standard year/Legends|year]] or less" after ''[[Lost Tribe of the Sith: Pandemonium]]''. Since ''Pandemonium'' is set in [[2975 BBY]], according to ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'', the events of ''Spiral'' 1—and by extension the events of the immediately following issue, [[Lost Tribe of the Sith — Spiral 2|''Lost Tribe of the Sith—Spiral'' 2]]—must be set between 2975 and [[2974 BBY]].</ref> the [[Lost Tribe of Sith|Sith tribe]] [[Prince/Legends|princess]] [[Takara Hilts]] inspected an image of a [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|lightsaber duel]] that was carved into the [[Water/Legends|ice]] walls of the [[Hall of Regret]] on the planet [[Kesh/Legends|Kesh]]. The hall was maintained by [[the Doomed]], a group of descendants of reconciled Jedi and Dark Jedi, and one of their members, [[Kaliska (S'kytri)|Kaliska]], retold the history of the Doomed beginning with the Battle of Corbos, listing XoXaan as one of the mighty Dark Jedi combatants there.<ref name="Spiral 2" />

[[File:XoXaanMeetsHett.jpg|thumb|right|250px|XoXaan's spirit and her new apprentice, A'Sharad Hett]]
XoXaan's spirit waited<ref name="Legacy 17">[[Legacy (2006) 17|''Legacy'' (2006) 17]]</ref> millennia more, until around [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]],<ref name="Dates" /> with the arrival of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]–turned–[[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Darth Krayt|A'Sharad Hett]] on Korriban. From the depths of her tomb, having sensed Hett's presence while he tracked a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] named [[Resk]], XoXaan's darkness called out to Hett's own darkness after he killed his target. XoXaan's voice beckoned Hett to follow, leading him to XoXaan's Temple, where he found the door to her burial chamber. After Hett [[Shatterpoint/Legends|shattered]] it with the Force, he discovered XoXaan's holocron, which released her spirit upon activation. Sensing the darkness within the bounty hunter, the spirit of XoXaan introduced itself to Hett and offered to initiate him in the ways of the dark side, an offer he accepted.<ref name="Legacy 17" />

As his [[Sith Master/Legends|Master]], XoXaan's spirit [[Sith training|taught]] Hett for years, instilling in him the traditions of the original Sith Lords and enhancing his skills through use of the dark side. However, Hett did not initially intend to fully embrace the dark side, and tried to convince himself that he could feign his status as a [[Sith acolyte/Legends|Sith Acolyte]]. He only wanted to learn enough about the dark side through XoXaan's teachings to further his own goals, which included toppling the perpetrator of the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]] and reigning Dark Lord of the Sith—[[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Sidious, and his apprentice, [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]].<ref name="leg18">[[Legacy (2006) 18|''Legacy'' (2006) 18]]</ref>

===Legacy===
{{Quote|He created himself, then re-created the Sith Order under the guidance of XoXaan—an ancient Sith warrior—his first and greatest teacher.|Darth Talon, regarding Darth Krayt|<ref name="leg17" />}}
[[File:XoXaan_Statue.jpg|thumb|left|180px|A statue of XoXaan at the Temple of the Sith]]
When Hett completed his training under XoXaan, he exited her tomb prepared to execute his mission against Darth Sidious, only to find that a [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] had replaced Sidious's [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Dejected, Hett left known space, where he was captured in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] and imprisoned by [[Scout/Legends|scouts]] from the [[Extragalactic/Legends|extra-galactic]] [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Species/Legends|species]], who also [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] and implanted their [[Yorik-Kul|slave seeds]] in him. Combined with XoXaan's teachings, those experiences led to his rebirth as Darth Krayt and the formation of his [[One Sith]] [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Order]]. After Krayt [[Battle of Coruscant (Sith–Imperial War)|conquered]] the capital world of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]<ref name="leg18" /> in [[130 ABY]],<ref name="LegDate">''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' dates [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[Battle of Coruscant (Sith–Imperial War)|victory]] over [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and the events of [[Legacy (2006) 14|''Legacy'' (2006) 14]] to [[130 ABY]] and [[137 ABY]], respectively.</ref> he placed a gigantic statue of XoXaan—which overlooked a [[Fire/Legends|fiery]] pit and held up a luminous ball within the new [[Temple of the Sith]]—in honor of his [[Teacher/Legends|mentor]].<ref name="leg18" />

By [[137 ABY]],<ref name="LegDate" /> the Yuuzhan Vong implants within Krayt had ravaged his body, to the point that he felt as though his death was on the horizon. [[Darth Wyyrlok (III)|Darth Wyyrlok]], Krayt's chief lieutenant, delved deep into ancient Sith lore hoping to find a way to cure his Master. He even consulted XoXaan's considerable knowledge of dark side healing for any potential secrets; however, Wyyrlok found that XoXaan's teachings proved unsatisfactory<ref name="leg14">[[Legacy (2006) 14|''Legacy'' (2006) 14]]</ref> and was thus forced to search elsewhere for answers.<ref name="leg27">[[Legacy (2006) 27|''Legacy'' (2006) 27]]</ref> Meanwhile, as part of former Jedi [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Cade Skywalker]]'s seduction and training to become a Sith, Krayt's own apprentice, [[Darth Talon]], taught Skywalker of how Krayt came to embrace the dark side after he met XoXaan and later the Yuuzhan Vong.<ref name="leg17" />

By that same [[Time/Legends|time]], XoXaan's temple on Korriban had been restored to a habitable capacity and was among other structures that were reconditioned and put to use by the One Sith. Her temple was furnished with stasis chambers and laboratories; its medical facilities would be used to rehabilitate several Sith Lords, who had all suffered grievous wounds and subsequent defeat in [[Battle on Had Abbadon|battle]] on the world [[Had Abbadon]].<ref name="leg34">[[Legacy (2006) 34|''Legacy'' (2006) 34]]</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|I am ''XoXaan''. I was one of the ''first'' of the Sith Lords, one of those who abandoned the Jedi Order to seek a darker path to truth.|XoXaan|<ref name="leg17" />}}
XoXaan was a [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] with spindly [[Limb/Legends|fingers]], free-flowing strands of [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]], gray [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and white skin. Her face sported symmetrical markings of a dark reddish-brown color, with two spots on the forehead, a pair of teardrop-like markings below the eyes, and pigments extending down from the lips and on the chin. Two streaks of pigments clung onto the humanoid's cheeks, although ornaments took the place of those markings in XoXaan's spirit form.<ref name="leg30" />

[[File:XoXaan-holocron.jpg|thumb|right|240px|XoXaan's holocron, which contained her Sith teachings]]
One of the first and most influencial of the Sith Lords,<ref name="CartelMarket2017">{{TORweb|url=info/news/news-article/20170222|text=Cartel Market Specials: Week of February 20, 2017}}</ref> XoXaan was a warrior who found the way of the Jedi unsatisfying. She believed that the teachings of the Jedi were flawed and that a greater truth lay in the path of the dark side. She also felt that [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] was empty and that Sith-inspired rule was required to fill it.<ref name="leg17" /> When the Dark Jedi encountered the native Sith on Korriban, XoXaan was among those who played off of their naiveté, allowing the Sith to treat them as deities.<ref name="leg30" />

She also adopted their customs, including the creation of holocrons, a secret that could be traced back to the ancient Sith [[King/Legends|king]] [[Adas]].<ref name="END">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/88/indexp2.html|text=''Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties''|int=Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties}}</ref> As a Sith Master, XoXaan reserved her teachings for those whom she believed had experienced her same disillusion with the Jedi Order, one who believed that the truth lay in the path of the dark side.<ref name="leg17" /> Sorzus Syn criticized XoXaan as a stubborn individual after the latter took away a Sith artifact that Syn had intended to study.<ref name="BOS" />

==Powers and abilities==
{{Quote|My Lord is dying. He is being consumed. Nothing in the Sith lore helps, including the ancient knowledge of XoXaan.|Darth Wyyrlok|<ref name="leg14" />}}
[[File:Black_Legions-BOS.png|thumb|left|300px|Marchioness XoXaan's charging Black Legions at the Battle of Corbos]]
A [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] member of the Jedi Order who fell to the dark side of the Force, XoXaan was noted as a distinguished Dark Jedi by Syn. As with the Black Legions that she commanded during the Battle of Corbos, Marchioness<ref name="BOS" /> XoXaan took part in [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]] against the Jedi loyal to the Republic, fighting with a [[Lightsaber/Legends|protosaber]].<ref name="Spiral 2" />

While the Dark Jedi forces were defeated at Corbos, XoXaan was one of twelve who survived the ordeal.<ref name="BOS" /> The fallen Jedi went on to use the power of the dark side to conquer beings she considered inferior, such as the malleable Sith race she and her fellow Exiles enslaved on Korriban. XoXaan was well-versed in Sith lore, and stored what she knew within the recesses of her own holocron.<ref name="leg17" /> Her knowledge included dark side healing techniques, although Darth Wyyrlok found that none of them were able to successfully remove the slave seeds from [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] Krayt's body.<ref name="leg27" />

==Equipment==
{{Quote|I had meant to study this treasure XoXaan stubbornly seized it for herself.|Sorzus Syn, on the Yoke of Seeming, ''Book of Sith''|<ref name="BOS" />}}
As a Dark Jedi battling on Corbos, XoXaan wielded a yellow-bladed protosaber and wore a modest set of black, gray, and white [[Clothing/Legends|clothes]].<ref name="Spiral 2" /> As a Sith Lord on Korriban, XoXaan was clothed in a flowing white dress with arm and neck wraps. She also wore an ornamental golden [[Vambrace/Legends|vambrace]] with three black spikes on her right arm.<ref name="leg30" />

The Sith Lord wore the same clothing in her spirit form,<ref name="leg17" /> as did the XoXaan statue at the Temple of the Sith, albeit with a hood up.<ref name="leg30" /> In the illustrations included in Sorzus Syn's chronicle, XoXaan was depicted as a Sith Lord on Korriban wearing a white overcoat with black linings, brandishing a red-bladed lightsaber. XoXaan eventually possessed the Yoke of Seeming, which allowed the wearer to appear as any person or object through illusion.<ref name="BOS" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|<nowiki />''[…]'' the Charge of the Black Legions at the Battle of Corbos, a new incident that presumably ties in with XoXaan's backstory as the commander of the Black Legions.|Daniel Wallace|<ref name="BOS endnotes">{{Blogspot|subdomain=geekosity|url=2012/03/endnotes-for-star-wars-book-of-sith.html|name=Daniel Wallace's Geekosity|text=Endnotes for Star Wars: Book of Sith (part 1)}}</ref>}}
XoXaan was first mentioned in the [[Legacy (2006) 14|fourteenth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy]]'' series, co-written by [[John Ostrander]] and [[Jan Duursema]] and published by [[Dark Horse Comics]]<ref name="leg14" /> on [[July 11]], [[2007]].<ref name="Marvel14">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54386/star_wars_legacy_2006_14|text=Star Wars: Legacy (2006) #14}}</ref> As there was no context given in the comic as to what XoXaan was—or even how the word was capitalized—questions arose on the [[TheForce.net|TheForce.Net]] [[Jedi Council Forums|forums]] on who or what it meant, and what the spelling and correct pronunciation was. Duursema established that the name was capitalized as "XoXaan", and that the pronunciation was "Zoh-Zaahn."<ref name="JCF">{{JCFCite|url=posts/27109830|board=Literature|thread=Legacy #14: Claws of the Dragon, part 1 (of 6)|user=[[Jan Duursema|Jan_Duursema]]|date=2007-07-12|time=5:44 PM|archiveurl=https://archive.ph/Xorxg}}</ref> XoXaan first appeared via holocron in a flashback sequence in ''Legacy''{{'s}} [[Legacy (2006) 17|seventeenth issue]], co-written by Ostrander and Duursema, illustrated by the latter, and published by Dark Horse Comics<ref name="leg17" /> on [[October 31]], 2007.<ref name="Marvel17">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54389/star_wars_legacy_2006_17|text=Star Wars: Legacy (2006) #17}}</ref>

[[File:XoXaan-TOR.jpg|thumb|right|300px|A promotional image showcasing XoXaan's outfit in ''Star Wars: The Old Republic'']]
When reviewing an illustration featured in his [[2012]] reference book ''[[Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side]]'', author [[Daniel Wallace]] stated that the depiction of the charge of the Black Legions at the Battle of Corbos could be a part of XoXaan's history as the Black Legions' commander.<ref name="BOS endnotes" />

In the [[2011]] [[BioWare]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]], ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]''{{'s}} Update 3.2, which was launched as part of the [[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Shadow of Revan|''Shadow of Revan'']] expansion in [[2015]], XoXaan's white outfit—complete with her golden vambrace—was introduced as a clothing item<ref name="Jedipedia">{{WebCite|url=https://swtor.jedipedia.net/en/itm/xoxaans-armor-set|text=Xoxaan's Armor Set – Items|work=[https://swtor.jedipedia.net Jedipedia.net]|archivedate=20211016174944}} — XoXaan's Armor Set was introduced in Update 3.2, which was released in [[2015]] and corresponds to the [[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Shadow of Revan|''Shadow of Revan'']] expansion, according to {{TORweb|url=patchnotes/4282015/game-update-3.2-rise-emperor|text=Game Update 3.2: Rise of the Emperor Patch Notes}}.</ref> available for purchase in-game via the [[Cartel Market]] feature. Articles promoting the clothing set on the game's [https://swtor.com official website] miscapitalize the Sith Lord's name as "Xoxaan."<ref name="CartelMarket2017" /><ref name="CartelMarket2018">{{TORweb|url=info/news/news-article/20180306|text=#SWTOR Cartel Market Specials: Week of March 5, 2018}}</ref>

==Appearances==
{{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of XoXaan}}
*''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' {{Mo}}
*''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Shadow of Revan]]'' {{C|Outfit}}
